title: Count Characters in a Sentence
description: Write a program that asks the user to input a sentence, then calculates and outputs how many characters there are in the sentence (excluding or including spaces, up to you).
solution:
text = input("Enter a sentence: ")
print(len(text))

title: Odd Numbers from 1 to 99
description: Write a program that outputs all odd numbers between 1 and 99 (inclusive).
solution:
for i in range(1, 100, 2):
    print(i, end=" ")

title: File Line Counter
description: Ask the user for a filename, then open the file and count how many lines it contains. Print the result.
example:
Input: test.txt
Output:
File has 12 lines.
solution:
filename = input("Enter a filename: ")
count = 0
with open(filename, 'r') as f:
    for line in f:
        count += 1
print(f"File has {count} lines.")

title: Character Frequency in String
description: Ask the user for a string and print the frequency of each character (excluding spaces).

title: Factorial Calculation
description: Ask the user for a number n and calculate n! (factorial) using a loop (not recursion).
solution:
n = int(input("Enter a number: "))
fact = 1
for i in range(1, n+1):
    fact *= i
print(fact)

title: Reverse Word Order
description: Ask the user for a sentence and print the words in reverse order.
example:
Input: Hello world program
Output:
program world Hello
solution:
text = input("Enter a sentence: ")
words = text.split()
reversed_words = words[::-1]
print(" ".join(reversed_words))

title: Simple Login System
description: Ask the user for a username and password, compare with stored values, and allow or deny access.
example:
Input: username: admin, password: 1234
Output:
Access granted!
solution:
stored_user = "admin"
stored_pass = "1234"
u = input("Enter username: ")
p = input("Enter password: ")
if u == stored_user and p == stored_pass:
    print("Access granted!")
else:
    print("Access denied!")

title: Longest Word in File
description: Ask the user for a filename, read the file, and find the longest word in the file.
example:
Input: test.txt
Output:
Longest word: programming

title: Recursive Palindrome Checker
description: Write a function to check if a string is a palindrome using recursion. Then ask the user for a word and use the function.

title: Temperature Converter Function
description: Write a function to convert Celsius to Fahrenheit and Fahrenheit to Celsius. Ask the user which conversion to perform, then ask for a value and print the result.
example:
Input: Convert C->F? C
Enter temperature: 0
Output:
32.0 F

title: Read & Write Text File
description: Ask the user for some text, write it to a file (e.g., 'output.txt'), then read the file and print the contents.
example:
Input: Hello file
Output:
Contents of output.txt: Hello file
solution:
text = input("Enter some text: ")
with open("output.txt", "w") as f:
    f.write(text)
with open("output.txt", "r") as f:
    data = f.read()
print("Contents of output.txt:", data)

title: Count Word Occurrences
description: Ask the user for a sentence and print how many times each word appears (case-insensitive).

title: Leap Year Checker
description: Ask the user for a year and determine if it is a leap year or not (divisible by 4, but not by 100 unless also by 400).

title: Greatest Common Divisor (GCD)
description: Ask the user for two numbers and calculate their GCD using the Euclidean algorithm.
solution:
def gcd(a, b):
    while b != 0:
        a, b = b, a % b
    return a
a = int(input("Enter first number: "))
b = int(input("Enter second number: "))
print(gcd(a, b))

title: Simple Dictionary Database
description: Ask the user to input multiple 'key=value' pairs. Store them in a dictionary and print the dictionary at the end.

title: Sum of Digits
description: Ask the user for a number and calculate the sum of its digits (e.g., 1234 -> 10).

title: File Copy
description: Ask the user for two filenames: a source and a destination. Copy the contents of the source file to the destination file.
example:
Input: source: test.txt, dest: backup.txt
Output:
File copied successfully.
solution:
src = input("Enter source filename: ")
dest = input("Enter destination filename: ")
with open(src, "r") as fsrc:
    data = fsrc.read()
with open(dest, "w") as fdest:
    fdest.write(data)
print("File copied successfully.")

title: Reverse File Content
description: Read a text file and create another file with the lines in reverse order.
example:
Input: file1.txt (with lines A, B, C)
Output: file2.txt (with lines C, B, A)
solution:
src = input("Enter source filename: ")
dest = input("Enter destination filename: ")
with open(src, "r") as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
lines.reverse()
with open(dest, "w") as f:
    f.writelines(lines)
print("File reversed.")

title: Temperature Logger
description: Ask the user for a temperature reading and append it to a file named temps.txt with a timestamp.
solution:
import datetime
reading = input("Enter temperature: ")
now = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
with open("temps.txt", "a") as f:
    f.write(f"{now}: {reading}\n")
print(f"Appended '{now}: {reading}' to temps.txt")

title: Define a Function to Check Perfect Number
description: A perfect number is one that is the sum of its proper divisors (e.g., 6 = 1+2+3). Define a function to check if a number is perfect, then ask the user for a number and test it.

title: Count Vowels, Consonants, Spaces
description: Ask the user for a sentence and print how many vowels, consonants, and spaces it has.
example:
Input: Hello world
Output:
Vowels=3, Consonants=7, Spaces=1

title: Basic ATM System
description: Simulate an ATM: the user can deposit, withdraw, or check balance. Store the balance in a variable (or file) and update it accordingly.
example:
Input: D 100, B -> Output: Balance=100
Input: W 20, B -> Output: Balance=80
Input: Q -> stops

title: Bubble Sort Visualization
description: Ask the user for a list of numbers, then perform bubble sort, printing the list after each pass.

title: Check Anagrams
description: Ask the user for two strings and check if they are anagrams (contain the same letters in a different order). Ignore spaces and case.

title: Recursive Fibonacci
description: Write a function to print the Fibonacci sequence up to a user-input number of terms, using recursion.
